item.title: Lancor Holdings Ltd Falls 19.29%: 5 Key Factors Driving the Sharp Decline
item.description: Lancor Holdings Ltd endured a challenging week from 09 to 13 March 2026, with its stock price plummeting 19.29% from ₹23.90 to ₹19.29, significantly underperforming the Sensex’s 4.87% decline over the same period. The stock repeatedly hit lower circuit limits amid heavy selling pressure, reflecting deteriorating fundamentals, weak technical indicators, and waning investor confidence in this micro-cap realty player. Time: 46 minutes ago

item.title: Jindal Saw Ltd Gains 14.79%: 5 Key Factors Driving the Week’s Momentum
item.description: Jindal Saw Ltd delivered a strong weekly performance from 9 to 13 March 2026, rising 14.79% from Rs.166.65 to Rs.191.30, significantly outperforming the Sensex which declined 4.87% over the same period. The stock’s rally was marked by exceptional volume surges, intraday highs, and notable institutional participation amid a broadly weak market and sector environment. Time: 46 minutes ago

item.title: Nelcast Ltd Gains 6.07%: 2 Key Factors Driving the Weekly Rally
item.description: Nelcast Ltd delivered a strong weekly performance, rising 6.07% from Rs.115.35 to Rs.122.35 between 9 and 13 March 2026, significantly outperforming the Sensex which declined 4.87% over the same period. The stock’s resilience amid a broadly negative market was supported by an upgrade to a Hold rating and stabilising technical momentum, reflecting improving fundamentals and cautious investor optimism. Time: 46 minutes ago

item.title: Aviva Industries Gains 4.03%: 3 Key Factors Driving the Weekly Rally
item.description: Aviva Industries Ltd delivered a notable weekly gain of 4.03%, closing at Rs.64.35 on 13 Mar 2026, in stark contrast to the Sensex’s 4.87% decline over the same period. The stock’s resilience was marked by two consecutive new 52-week highs and an upgrade in its Mojo Grade, underscoring a week of positive momentum amid a broadly weakening market environment. Time: 46 minutes ago

item.title: ICICI Bank Ltd Declines 4.50%: 5 Key Factors Shaping This Week’s Market Action
item.description: ICICI Bank Ltd. closed the week ending 13 March 2026 at Rs.1,254.30, down 4.50% from the previous Friday’s close of Rs.1,313.35. This decline slightly outperformed the Sensex’s 4.87% fall over the same period, reflecting a challenging week marked by technical weakness, high trading volumes, and cautious investor sentiment amid broader market volatility. Time: 46 minutes ago

item.title: Kajaria Ceramics Declines 2.72% Amid Downgrade and Bearish Technical Momentum
item.description: Kajaria Ceramics Ltd. closed the week ending 13 March 2026 at Rs.910.10, down 2.72% from Rs.935.50 the previous Friday, while the Sensex declined 4.87% over the same period. Despite the stock’s modest outperformance relative to the broader market, the week was marked by a series of downgrades and technical shifts signalling growing caution among investors amid valuation concerns and bearish momentum. Time: 46 minutes ago

item.title: Swan Defence Surges 19.11% Amidst Record Highs and Volatile Trading
item.description: Swan Defence and Heavy Industries Ltd delivered a remarkable weekly gain of 19.11%, closing at Rs. 2,399.30 on 13 March 2026, significantly outperforming the Sensex which declined 4.87% over the same period. The stock’s strong momentum was marked by multiple new 52-week and all-time highs, hitting upper circuit limits twice, and sustained buying interest despite broader market weakness. This review analyses the key events and technical developments that shaped the stock’s performance during the week. Time: 46 minutes ago

item.title: Bayer CropScience Ltd Declines 1.95% Amid Downgrade and Mixed Technical Signals
item.description: Bayer CropScience Ltd closed the week at Rs.4,511.65, down 1.95% from the previous Friday’s close of Rs.4,601.45, while the Sensex declined more sharply by 4.87%. Despite the stock’s modest weekly loss, it outperformed the broader market index, reflecting a relatively resilient performance amid a challenging market environment marked by technical downgrades and mixed financial signals. Time: 46 minutes ago

item.title: Southern Petrochemical Industries Ltd. Dips 1.94% Amid Volatile Week: Key Price Swings and Market Context
item.description: Southern Petrochemical Industries Corporation Ltd. (SPIC) experienced a volatile week ending 13 March 2026, closing at Rs.60.22, down 1.94% from the previous Friday’s close of Rs.61.41. Despite this modest decline, SPIC outperformed the broader Sensex, which fell 4.87% over the same period. The week was marked by a fresh 52-week low on 9 March and a sharp intraday rebound on 10 March, reflecting mixed investor sentiment amid challenging market conditions. Time: 46 minutes ago

item.title: Jindal Stainless Ltd Falls 5.76%: Key Valuation and Technical Shifts Shape the Week
item.description: Jindal Stainless Ltd experienced a volatile week from 9 to 13 March 2026, ending with a 5.76% decline to close at Rs.708.15, slightly underperforming the Sensex’s 4.87% fall. The stock faced significant intraday lows amid sectoral and market-wide selling pressures but also saw a notable upgrade in its valuation and technical outlook midweek, reflecting a complex interplay of short-term weakness and improving fundamentals. Time: 46 minutes ago

item.title: Orient Cement Ltd. Drops 8.30% Amid Mixed Signals and Market Pressure
item.description: Orient Cement Ltd. endured a challenging week from 9 to 13 March 2026, with its stock price declining sharply by 8.30% to close at Rs.133.10, significantly underperforming the Sensex’s 4.87% fall. The week was marked by fresh 52-week lows, mixed technical signals, and a cautious upgrade to a Hold rating by MarketsMOJO, reflecting a complex interplay of improving fundamentals amid persistent market and sectoral headwinds. Time: 47 minutes ago

item.title: Vinyl Chemicals Declines 7.86% This Week: Key Drivers Behind the 52-Week Lows
item.description: Vinyl Chemicals (I) Ltd experienced a challenging week, with its stock price declining 7.86% from Rs.207.40 to Rs.191.10, underperforming the Sensex which fell 4.87% over the same period. The stock hit fresh 52-week lows twice during the week amid persistent financial headwinds and broader market weakness, despite a modest upgrade in valuation appeal midweek. Time: 47 minutes ago

item.title: Jindal Poly Investment & Finance Gains 11.89%: 3 Key Factors Driving the Surge
item.description: Jindal Poly Investment & Finance Company Ltd delivered a robust weekly performance, gaining 11.89% from Rs.1,101.35 to Rs.1,232.35, significantly outperforming the Sensex which declined 4.87% over the same period. The stock’s rally was marked by a new 52-week and all-time high on 10 March 2026, supported by strong valuation metrics, exceptional financial growth, and positive technical momentum amid a volatile broader market. Time: 47 minutes ago

item.title: Eicher Motors Declines 11.46%: 5 Key Factors Behind the Weekly Downtrend
item.description: Eicher Motors Ltd endured a challenging week from 9 to 13 March 2026, with its share price declining by 11.46% to close at Rs.6,745.25, significantly underperforming the Sensex’s 4.87% fall. The stock faced persistent selling pressure amid broader market weakness, sectoral headwinds, and increased bearish sentiment reflected in heavy put option activity. Despite some intraday rebounds, the overall trend was negative, with the stock touching multiple intraday lows and closing below key short-term moving averages. Time: 47 minutes ago

item.title: UltraTech Cement Declines 11.51%: 6 Key Factors Driving the Weekly Sell-Off
item.description: UltraTech Cement Ltd endured a challenging week from 9 to 13 March 2026, with its share price declining sharply by 11.51%, significantly underperforming the Sensex which fell 4.87% over the same period. The stock faced persistent selling pressure amid broader market weakness, technical headwinds, and valuation concerns, culminating in a series of intraday lows and a downgraded investment grade. Despite intermittent rebounds, the overall sentiment remained cautious as the stock traded below key moving averages throughout the week. Time: 47 minutes ago

item.title: Cyient Ltd Declines 1.65% Despite Valuation Appeal Amid Market Volatility
item.description: Cyient Ltd’s shares declined by 1.65% over the week ending 13 March 2026, closing at Rs.869.30, underperforming the Sensex which fell 4.87%. The stock experienced significant volatility, hitting a 52-week low early in the week before a midweek rebound driven by valuation upgrades. However, renewed selling pressure towards the week’s close capped gains, reflecting ongoing earnings concerns amid sector challenges. Time: 47 minutes ago

item.title: Swaraj Engines Gains 0.48%: 2 Key Valuation Shifts Shape the Week
item.description: Swaraj Engines Ltd recorded a modest weekly gain of 0.48%, closing at Rs.3,476.65 on 13 Mar 2026, outperforming the Sensex which declined 4.87% over the same period. The week was marked by significant valuation reassessments and steady operational performance, with the stock navigating a volatile market backdrop to maintain relative resilience. Time: 47 minutes ago

item.title: Indian Hotels Co Ltd Drops 2.23% Amid Sector and Market Weakness: 5 Key Insights
item.description: Indian Hotels Co Ltd’s stock declined by 2.23% over the week ending 13 March 2026, closing at Rs.609.80 from Rs.623.70. This underperformance came despite the broader Sensex falling 4.87%, indicating relative resilience amid sectoral and market headwinds. The week was marked by a fresh 52-week low on 9 March and persistent selling pressure, reflecting ongoing challenges in the Hotels, Resorts & Restaurants sector and heightened market volatility. Time: 48 minutes ago

item.title: Tata Consultancy Services Declines 5.76%: 10 Days of Pressure Amid Heavy Options Activity
item.description: Tata Consultancy Services Ltd. (TCS) endured a challenging week from 9 to 13 March 2026, with its stock price declining by 5.76% to close at Rs.2,410.30, marginally underperforming the Sensex’s 4.87% fall. The stock hit successive 52-week lows throughout the week amid sustained selling pressure, heavy options market activity, and bearish technical signals, reflecting a cautious market sentiment despite the company’s strong fundamental metrics. Time: 48 minutes ago

item.title: Chambal Fertilisers Falls 3.01% Amid Bearish Momentum and Sector Pressures
item.description: Chambal Fertilisers & Chemicals Ltd closed the week at Rs.415.45, down 3.01% from the previous Friday’s close of Rs.428.35, underperforming the Sensex which fell 4.87% over the same period. The stock faced significant volatility, hitting a 52-week low early in the week before technical indicators signalled sustained bearish momentum amid broader market weakness and sectoral pressures. Time: 48 minutes ago
